facilities.title: Jia Yuan Retirement Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Jia Yuan Retirement Care Centre is at Jalan PP1/6, Taman Putra Prima, Puchong — a residential township in the southwest Klang Valley, roughly equidistant between Puchong town and Putrajaya. The facility is registered with company number 202401017567, indicating incorporation in 2024 and making this one of the most recently established nursing homes in the Puchong corridor. The Taman Putra Prima neighbourhood is quieter and more suburban than the commercial centre of Puchong town, which can suit families looking for a less congested setting for a residential placement.

The recency of establishment means that Jia Yuan has a shorter operational track record than most nursing homes in the area and limited publicly available service detail. The 4.1-star Google rating from 17 reviews is a moderate result; the small review base means individual experiences carry significant weight in the average. Pricing is quoted on enquiry. Staffing structure, bed capacity and JKM licence details are not stated in public listings.

When you contact Jia Yuan, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and current status — facilities incorporated in 2024 may be at varying stages in the licensing cycle. Ask about the total bed capacity and current occupancy, the care types accepted — assisted living, nursing care, dementia, palliative, bedbound residents — and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Confirm the monthly pricing structure and whether medication, physiotherapy and incontinence supplies are included in the base fee. Ask about the founding operator's background and experience in elder care. Given the limited public profile, a site visit to meet the care team and inspect the premises in person is particularly important before committing to placement.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Little Sisters of the Poor (St Francis Xavier's)
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Little Sisters of the Poor — also known as St Francis Xavier's Home for the Elderly — is a Catholic charitable home for older adults at 3, Jalan 5/96 off Jalan Sekuci, Taman Sri Bahtera, Batu 5 Cheras, Kuala Lumpur. The Little Sisters of the Poor congregation first arrived in Malaysia in 1965, running homes on Klang Road and then Weld Road before moving into the present Cheras premises in November 1973. The congregation also operates a sister home in Penang; together the two Malaysian houses form part of the international Little Sisters of the Poor network, which has provided residential care for the elderly poor for over 180 years globally.

The institutional depth of this home is unusual in the Malaysian nursing home landscape. The Little Sisters congregation brings a globally established tradition of elder care founded explicitly on the welfare of the impoverished elderly rather than commercial provision. As a charitable home, the admission model and fee structure differ from private commercial nursing homes — placement is not simply a matter of paying a monthly rate, and eligibility criteria are shaped by the congregation's welfare mission. The 4.8-star Google rating from 17 reviews is strong and consistent with the home's long-standing community reputation in the Cheras area.

For families whose elderly relative may meet the home's welfare and eligibility criteria, or for Catholic families in the KL-Cheras corridor who value the faith context that St Francis Xavier's provides, the correct first step is to contact the home directly. Ask about the current admission process and eligibility criteria, the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence status, what level of nursing and medical care is available on-site, and whether placement is open to non-Catholic residents. The Cheras location is accessible from the Pandan, Ampang and inner Cheras corridor.

facilities.title: Loving Mansion Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Loving Mansion Care Centre occupies a cluster of shoplots on Jalan Kota 2/3 in Kawasan Perindustrian Kota Puteri, Masai — approximately 15 minutes from JB's main CIQ crossing and positioned in the growing eastern JB-Masai corridor. The operator is registered as CT0113053-M and reports more than 20 years of continuous operation in elderly care — a meaningful track record by any measure. Day-to-day operations are led by a manager known as Alan, who is cited by name in multiple family reviews, suggesting a consistent point of contact that families appreciate in the facility's communication style.

Services include assisted living, skilled nursing and rehabilitation. The home is bilingual in practice, operating in English and Mandarin, and serves a predominantly Chinese-Malaysian resident base. The published starting price of RM 2,500 per month is comparable to other mid-tier JB private nursing homes in the Kempas and Masai corridor. The 5.0-star Google rating from 17 reviews is clean, and the named-manager culture is often a marker of smaller homes where accountability and continuity of care are more personally embedded than in larger, more institutional facilities.

When you contact Loving Mansion, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and expiry, total bed count and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Clarify whether the RM 2,500 starting rate applies to shared accommodation and what higher pricing tiers look like. Ask which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and how clinical escalation is managed. The Masai location serves the eastern JB and Pasir Gudang corridor well; confirm visiting convenience before committing to placement.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Al-Fattah
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Emas Al-Fattah is a community care centre at No. 9, Jalan TTS 7/1, Taman Tasik Semenyih, 43500 Semenyih — a quiet lakeside housing estate approximately 45 minutes southeast of Kuala Lumpur in the Hulu Langat district. The centre was established in 2018 by Mohammad Azlan Abdul Rahman, growing from a grass-roots welfare initiative aimed at elderly individuals who lack family support or who are managing chronic conditions such as hypertension, diabetes or limited mobility. The home operates from a rented residential property, which is typical of community-scale welfare facilities that began informally before achieving formal registration.

The welfare origins of Al-Fattah shape both who it serves and how families should approach it. This is not a commercial nursing home with a straightforward fee-and-placement model; it is a community welfare centre with an Islamic character that grew from a personal mission to support the vulnerable elderly. The 4.4-star Google rating from 16 reviews is solid for a Semenyih-area welfare facility. Pricing is quoted on direct enquiry; families should clarify whether the fee structure is commercial, welfare-subsidised, or a mix. The Taman Tasik Semenyih lakeside setting is peaceful, which can suit residents who benefit from a calmer, natural environment.

For families in the Semenyih-Beranang-Kajang southern corridor looking for an affordable residential care option with an Islamic character, Al-Fattah is one of the few locally rooted options in the area. When you contact the home, ask about the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence status, the total number of beds and current occupancy, nursing qualifications and overnight staffing, and the fee structure. Ask which care conditions — dementia, bedbound, palliative — are accepted, and confirm whether the home accepts residents from outside the immediate Semenyih area.

facilities.title: Evergreen ElderCare Centre
facilities.editorial:
Evergreen ElderCare Centre has been operating in Taman Johor, Johor Bahru, since 2014 and is one of the few nursing homes in Johor that publicly anchors its dementia care in a Montessori-for-Dementia framework — a structured approach that uses meaningful daily activities to maintain cognitive function and dignity rather than relying on sedation or passive custodial care. The centre also runs a programme it calls Greening Dementia. Communication across the operator's website and marketing is in both English and Mandarin, positioning the home for English and Mandarin-speaking Chinese Malaysian families in JB and across the Johor corridor.

The Montessori-for-Dementia positioning is worth taking seriously. While many nursing homes in Malaysia list dementia care among their services without describing the approach, Evergreen's explicit public commitment to a named evidence-based framework suggests a degree of deliberate clinical investment in dementia management. Families with a relative experiencing cognitive decline should ask to verify what specific training and certification the care team holds in Montessori-for-Dementia practice. The 4.8-star Google rating from 16 reviews is strong for a JB home in this review band. Services include 24-hour nursing and respite care. Pricing is quoted on direct enquiry.

When you contact Evergreen ElderCare Centre, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and expiry date, the total bed count and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Ask specifically about the Montessori-for-Dementia programme — what formal training staff have completed, which activities are delivered daily, and how the programme is adapted for residents at different stages of cognitive decline. Confirm which other care conditions are accepted — palliative, PEG tube, post-stroke — and ask for a breakdown of the pricing schedule including what the base monthly fee covers.

facilities.title: Golden Cares Nursing Home
facilities.editorial:
Golden Cares Nursing Home occupies a shophouse at No. 25, Jalan Changgai 6/22, Seksyen 6, Petaling Jaya — a mature residential neighbourhood with good access to hospital facilities along the Klang Valley corridor. The facility was established in 1999, giving it over 25 years of continuous operation in the PJ market — a meaningful track record that few community-scale nursing homes in the area can match. The disclosed bed count is 25, placing it in the small, community-home category where care tends to be more personal than in a large institutional facility.

The 4.3-star Google rating from 15 reviews is a steady mid-range result for a PJ-area nursing home of this size and age. The Facebook presence under the name 'goldencare' indicates the operator maintains some public communications, though detailed service information is not published. Pricing is not publicly stated. The shophouse format at Seksyen 6 is worth noting: converted shophouses are practical for ambulatory residents but families with a relative who requires ceiling hoist access or a powered wheelchair should confirm the physical layout in advance.

When you contact Golden Cares, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and current status, the current number of residents and remaining capacity, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Ask which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and how clinical escalation is handled after hours. Confirm the monthly pricing structure and what is included in the base fee. At 25 beds, understanding who is on duty during the overnight shift is particularly important, and a site visit before committing to placement is recommended.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Rumah Warga Tua Cahaya
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Rumah Warga Tua Cahaya is at No. 2, Jalan Merpati 8, Puchong Jaya, and has been in operation since 2010. The facility is classified as an associations-type care provider rather than a corporate nursing home chain, with a disclosed bed count of 76 — making it one of the larger residential care facilities in the Puchong Jaya sub-market, where most nursing homes operate at 20 to 40 beds. At 76 beds, the home sits in a category where staffing ratios, clinical governance and operational systems carry significantly more weight than at smaller community homes.

The 4.5-star Google rating from 15 reviews is solid. The associations classification suggests the home may operate with some welfare or community-funding element alongside or instead of full commercial fee income, though the exact funding model is not stated publicly. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct contact. The Puchong Jaya location is well connected for families in the Puchong-Subang Jaya-Bukit Jalil corridor and within reasonable reach of Sunway Medical Centre and several public hospitals.

When you contact Rumah Warga Tua Cahaya, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and expiry, clarification of whether the home operates as a fully commercial facility or with welfare-funded admissions, confirmation of the actual current occupancy and whether all parts of the facility are in active use, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. At 76 beds, the overnight staffing structure is a particularly significant variable to probe. Ask which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and confirm the monthly pricing structure and what the base fee covers. A site visit to see the full facility is recommended before committing.

facilities.title: Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Hayati Care Centre
facilities.editorial:
Pusat Jagaan Warga Tua Hayati Care Centre serves the Batu Pahat district of Johor — a secondary city of around 400,000 people roughly 100 km southwest of Johor Bahru. Residential care options in Batu Pahat district are significantly more limited than in the JB urban corridor; for families based in Batu Pahat, Muar or the surrounding Johor southwest, a facility like Hayati removes the need to transport a dependent elderly relative into the congested JB market where visiting distances become impractical. The home is listed as a nursing home on Google Maps and specific address details are available through direct contact.

The 4.9-star Google rating from 15 reviews is among the stronger scores in the Batu Pahat eldercare market, where review pools are smaller than in urban centres due to lower population density and slower adoption of online review platforms among the older demographic. For families in the district, a home with a near-perfect score from local community members is a positive signal worth noting. Pricing is quoted on direct enquiry. Physical details of the facility — bed count, staff qualifications, premises layout — are not available from public listings.

When you contact Hayati Care Centre, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and its current status, the total bed count and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for overnight shifts. Confirm which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, post-stroke bedbound residents — and how the home handles medical escalation given the distance from JB's major specialist hospitals. Ask which is the nearest hospital to the facility and what the typical response time looks like. Confirm the monthly pricing structure. A site visit before placement is recommended, especially for families travelling from outside Batu Pahat to evaluate the home.

facilities.title: Unique Family Care Center
facilities.editorial:
Unique Family Care Center is at 2 Jalan PU 1/10A, Taman Puchong Utama, Puchong — a landed residential estate in the northeastern part of Puchong, roughly midway between Puchong Jaya and the Bukit Jalil corridor. Google Maps lists the facility as both a nursing home and a senior citizen centre, and the 'family' emphasis in the name is consistent with a home-like, smaller-scale facility operating out of a converted residential property. The residential address and branding suggest an environment intentionally less institutional than larger nursing home buildings in the area.

The 4.9-star Google rating from 15 reviews is strong and consistent. Pricing is not published and is quoted on direct enquiry. Bed capacity, nursing qualifications and JKM licence details are not available from public listings. The Taman Puchong Utama location gives families in the Puchong-Bukit Jalil-Sri Petaling corridor a conveniently located option, and the neighbourhood's landed-residential character typically means less traffic and easier parking for visiting family members compared to shophouse-belt nursing homes.

When you contact Unique Family Care Center, ask for the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence number and its current validity status, total bed capacity and current occupancy, and the nurse-to-resident ratio for day and overnight shifts. If the facility operates out of a converted residential house, ask specifically about lift access or hoist provision for residents with limited mobility, and confirm that bathroom configuration is accessible for wheelchair users. Ask which care conditions are accepted — dementia, palliative, PEG tube, bedbound residents — and confirm the monthly pricing structure including what the base fee covers. A site visit to see the room layout and shared spaces is recommended before placement.

facilities.title: Rumah Orang Tua Charis
facilities.editorial:
Rumah Orang Tua Charis is a Christian welfare home in Taman Lucky, Kuala Lumpur, founded in 1988 by Reverend Teo How Ken and formally registered with the Registrar of Societies in 1993. With 17 elderly residents, this is a very small, community-embedded home operating as part of a broader welfare campus that includes a children's home under the same organisation. Weekly services are held at the affiliated Methodist Charis Community Care Center, and a social programme called the VSOP Club — Very Strong Old People — meets on Thursday mornings, bringing in community members for regular structured socialisation with the residents.

The scale and character of this home are deeply embedded in the Methodist Christian community context. As a welfare organisation rather than a commercial nursing home, Charis is not a fee-for-service facility in the conventional sense; its residents are part of a faith community, and the home's intake reflects that mission. The 4.3-star Google rating from 14 reviews reflects the community members, church volunteers and social workers who interact regularly with the home rather than families making a commercial nursing home comparison. No pricing is published, consistent with a welfare model.

For families connected to the Methodist community in KL, or for individuals who may qualify under the home's charitable admission criteria, a direct approach is the right first step. Ask about the current admission process and eligibility criteria, the JKM Akta Pusat Jagaan licence or welfare registration status, and what level of nursing and medical care is available for residents with higher-dependency needs. Given the 17-resident scale, the home's clinical capacity is likely suited to lower-dependency elderly care; families with a relative requiring full nursing oversight, palliative care or complex medical management should confirm this carefully before pursuing a placement.
